title: Open And Cycle Tab Pages
goal: Open a new tab with `:tabnew`, then cycle back with `gT`.
explanation: Tab pages group window layouts. `:tabnew` opens a new tab, `gt` advances, `gT` reverses, `:tabclose` drops the current one.
hints: Run :tabnew and press Enter — a fresh tab opens.
Press gT to move back to the original tab.
rule: Use tabs when one window layout doesn't capture what you're doing.

check.success_text: You opened a tab and walked the tab list with gT.

title: Close A Tab
goal: Open a tab with `:tabnew`, then close it with `:tabclose`.
explanation: `:tabclose` (or `:tabc`) drops the current tab and returns you to its neighbor.
hints: Use :tabnew to open the second tab.
rule: Open a tab when you need a side-channel layout; close it when the work is done.

check.success_text: You opened and then closed a tab page cleanly.

title: Reorder Windows With Ctrl-W
goal: Open a `:split`, then move the active window to the end of the list with `<C-w>L`.
explanation: `<C-w>H/J/K/L` rearrange splits. In real Vim they shift orientation; the trainer reorders the window list so layout-management lessons feel coherent.
hints: Run :split to create a second window.
Press Ctrl-W then L to push the active window to the last slot.
rule: Layout reordering is a Ctrl-W command; sizes are a separate concern.

check.success_text: You created a split and moved the active window to the end of the layout.

title: Keep Only One Window
goal: Open two splits, then collapse to a single window with `<C-w>o`.
explanation: `<C-w>o` (`only-window`) drops every window except the active one. Useful when you've split too many times and want a fresh layout.
hints: Run :split twice to make three windows.
Press Ctrl-W then o to keep only the current window.
rule: When the layout gets noisy, <C-w>o resets it without losing the buffer.

check.success_text: You collapsed multiple windows down to one with <C-w>o.

title: Reindent A Single Line
goal: Use `==` to align the misindented middle line to the surrounding indent.
explanation: `==` reindents the current line. `=ip` reindents an inner paragraph. The trainer aligns every targeted line to the indent of the first non-empty line in range.
hints: Move down to the misindented middle line with j.
rule: Use == when one line drifts out of column with its neighbors.

check.success_text: You realigned a stray line to the surrounding indent with ==.

title: Reindent An Inner Paragraph
goal: Use `=ip` to reflow indentation across the paragraph.
explanation: `=ip` runs the reindent operator over the inner-paragraph text object. Every line in the paragraph gets aligned to the first line's indent.
hints: The cursor starts inside the paragraph.
Press =ip to reindent the whole paragraph at once.
rule: Operator + text-object scales: =ip is to == what dap is to dd.

check.success_text: You reindented an entire paragraph in a single operator + text-object pair.

title: Reflow A Paragraph To Textwidth
goal: Set `:set textwidth=20`, then use `gqip` to reflow the paragraph.
explanation: `gq` wraps text to `textwidth`. The default is 78; setting it tighter (here, 20) makes the wrap visible in a small lesson buffer.
hints: Then press gqip to reflow the paragraph at the cursor.
rule: Set textwidth, then let gq do the wrapping; manual <CR> insertion is rarely the right move.

buffers.name: doc.md
lines: alpha beta gamma delta epsilon zeta eta theta

check.success_text: You set textwidth and let gqip wrap the paragraph for you.
